title: Harden injection detection against obfuscated, encoded, and indirect prompt injection
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 — LLM01 Prompt Injection (indirect/multilingual/encoded vectors); Unicode homoglyph & base64 smuggling research.
description: detect_injection_patterns() in security_utils.py:574 is regex-only and English-only against raw text, so it misses the dominant 2025 attack vectors: base64/hex/rot13-encoded payloads, Unicode homoglyphs and zero-width characters that defeat the regex, and indirect injection arriving via file/tool/git-diff content fed into prompts. Morty's read repository files and git diffs, which are untrusted indirect-injection surfaces not currently spotlighted.
proposed_changes: In security_utils.py: (1) normalize text with unicodedata.normalize('NFKC', ...) and strip zero-width/bidi control chars before pattern matching; (2) add a decode-and-rescan pass that base64/hex-decodes long token runs and re-runs INJECTION_PATTERNS on the decoded text; (3) add patterns for tool/data exfiltration cues ('print your system prompt', 'send to http', 'curl', 'read .env'). In delegate.py, route file contents and `git diff` output that get embedded into Morty prompts through wrap_untrusted_content() with label='REPO_CONTENT' so indirect injection from working-tree files is spotlighted, not trusted.
